destinations.evidence: AutonomousAgent and Telemetry are enum values but are intentionally absent from the executor-capability match set.
Local tests explicitly assert the service capabilities are not executor capabilities.

equivalence_argument: AutonomousAgent and Telemetry are decoded registry capabilities but not classified as executor capabilities.
notes: The comment still says autonomous agent executor capability, but the executable predicate and tests keep it out of executor filtering.
rationale: The tests add explicit assertions that AutonomousAgent and Telemetry return false for is_executor_capability, and the commit message says they should be kept out because they are service capabilities. The primary diff adds tests asserting both new capabilities return false from `is_executor_capability`, preserving the separation between executor service capabilities and registry/telemetry agent capabilities. Collapsed 2 closely related historical candidate statements into the latest effective formulation.
statement: `AutonomousAgent` and `Telemetry` capabilities must not be classified as executor capabilities by `Capability::is_executor_capability()`.
suggested_falsification_tests: Assert Capability::AutonomousAgent.is_executor_capability() and Capability::Telemetry.is_executor_capability() both return false while executor-serving capabilities continue to return true.

Assert `Capability::AutonomousAgent.is_executor_capability()` and `Capability::Telemetry.is_executor_capability()` both return false, while existing executor precompile capabilities still return true.
summary: Agent registry capabilities remain separate from executor capabilities.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Assert AutonomousAgent and Telemetry return false while Fhe/HttpCall/Audio/Video executor capabilities return true.
title: Agent registry capabilities are not executor capabilities
